title: Parallax Model Descriptor (m-descriptor)
description: Canonical, serialized form of the m-descriptor metamodel. A model descriptor is an instance of this schema. Grown one metamodel phase at a time; this revision covers entity + attribute + pkGenerator + relationship + index + asOfAttribute (the m-temporal-read temporal dimension) + inheritance (a closed class tree: roles root / abstract-subtype / concrete-subtype, root-only strategy table-per-hierarchy with a `tag`/`tagValue` discriminator or table-per-concrete-subtype; table-per-leaf and table-per-class are REJECTED, m-inheritance) + valueObject (a recursive, typed embedded composite — typed attributes, nested value objects to arbitrary depth, and one|many cardinality — mapped to a single dialect-native structured-document column per top-level value object via the m-core `json` neutral type, a deliberate deviation from Reladomo's column-flattening), and admits multiple entities per descriptor (a single `entity` for one-entity models, or an `entities` array for multi-entity models with relationships between them).

entities.description: Two or more entities mapped in one descriptor (so relationships can name sibling entities). Mutually exclusive with the single `entity` form.

neutralType.description: m-core neutral data type. The decimal type carries precision/scale, e.g. decimal(18,2). The `json` type is the embedded-value type a valueObject element maps to; m-dialect maps it to each dialect's structured-document column type.

valueObjects.description: Embedded composite elements mapped to dialect-native structured-document columns via the m-core `json` neutral type (m-value-object/m-core; a deliberate deviation from Reladomo's column-flattening).

allOf.$comment: Optimistic-lock composition (m-descriptor/m-temporal-read/m-opt-lock): a temporal (as-of) entity DERIVES its optimistic key from the processing-from column, so it MUST NOT also declare an explicit `optimisticLocking` version attribute. Combining `asOfAttributes` with an `optimisticLocking` attribute on one entity is invalid.

Conditional `table` requirement (m-inheritance / m-descriptor, resolved Q5): a non-inheritance entity, and a concrete subtype, own the physical table their rows live in, so `table` is REQUIRED. An abstract inheritance node (`role: root` or `abstract-subtype`) is tableless and rowless — its rows live in a concrete descendant's table — so `table` is FORBIDDEN there (the sibling `not` rule below).
An abstract inheritance node (`role: root` or `abstract-subtype`) is tableless and rowless (m-inheritance): it MUST NOT declare a physical `table`.

Conditional `attributes` requirement (m-inheritance / m-descriptor, resolved Q5): a non-inheritance entity MUST declare its own `attributes`. An inheritance participant MAY omit `attributes` — a concrete subtype that declares only inherited attributes has none of its own, and validation/lowering derives the full inherited chain from the ancestry. When `attributes` IS present it is still non-empty (the array schema).

At most one optimisticLocking attribute per entity (m-descriptor `attribute` / m-opt-lock "The version column"): the entity's OWN `attributes` array may CONTAIN at most one item with `optimisticLocking: true` (2020-12 `contains`/`minContains`/`maxContains`). `minContains: 0` is REQUIRED alongside `maxContains` — `contains` alone implies at least one match, which would wrongly force every entity to declare a version attribute; `minContains: 0` restores the flag's true optionality, so 0 or 1 both satisfy this, never more. This is a PER-ENTITY structural rule; the family-wide root-ownership rule (at most one across an inheritance family, declared only at the root) is the semantic `inheritance-optimistic-locking-not-root-owned` invariant a model-aware validator enforces (m-inheritance), not expressible here.

Explicit optimistic-lock version (m-opt-lock / DQ8b): an attribute-level `optimisticLocking: true` version is derived — initialized to `1` on insert and advanced to `observed + 1` on every update, gated in optimistic mode with `and <col> = ?` — so it MUST be an integral type (`int32`/`int64`). A TEMPORAL (as-of) entity locks via its processing `in_z` TIMESTAMP instead and declares no explicit version attribute (the entity-level composition rule above forbids the combination), so this restriction never lands on a timestamp column.

relationship.description: A navigable association. Joins are auto-derived from the navigation predicate, so a query never writes ON-conditions by hand.

reverseName.description: Optional reverse-relationship name on the related entity.

dependent.description: True when the target is owned by this entity (participates in cascade, m-cascade-delete).

foreignKey.description: Optional foreign-key hint (column on the many side).

orderBy.description: Optional ordering for a to-many relationship.

attributes.description: Ordered list of attribute names participating in the index.

unique.description: True for a unique index (enables the cache fast-path).

asOfAttribute.description: A temporal dimension (m-temporal-read): a query-time virtual attribute backed by a [from, to) interval over a pair of timestamp columns. An entity with one asOfAttribute is unitemporal (processing or business); with two it is bitemporal. The open upper bound (`to`) is the database-native infinity (m-core/m-dialect). Phase 5 (temporal MVP) exercises the single-axis `processing` (audit-only) profile.

fromColumn.description: The interval's inclusive lower-bound column (the `from`/`in` column).

toColumn.description: The interval's upper-bound column (the `to`/`out`/`thru` column); equals infinity for a current row.

axis.description: Which temporal axis this dimension models. `processing` is when the system knew a fact (audit / as-of-now); `business` is when the fact is true in the world.

toIsInclusive.description: Interval closure. false (default) ⇒ [from, to); true ⇒ [from, to].

infinity.description: The open-bound sentinel = the database-native infinity (the m-dialect dialect owns the concrete representation). Always `infinity` in the canonical descriptor; a dialect lacking native infinity maps it to a documented max-sentinel.

default.description: Default-if-unspecified for this dimension when a query omits it. `now` ⇒ as-of-the-current-instant (the current milestone, i.e. to = infinity). Only `now` is defined for the temporal MVP.

valueObject.description: A top-level embedded composite element declared on an entity and stored as a single dialect-native structured-document column (DQ9 definitely-do). Unlike Reladomo's column-flattening, the WHOLE value object — its typed `attributes` AND any nested `valueObjects`, to arbitrary depth — is stored in one m-core `json`-typed column; the concrete storage type is owned by m-dialect. Only a top-level value object carries storage (`column`/`mapping`); nested value objects (`nestedValueObject`) share the same column and carry none. Inner fields are read/filtered with the m-op-algebra nested-attribute access form (`Class.valueObject.path`).

column.description: The single structured-document column the whole value object (attributes + nested value objects) is stored in. Top-level only — a nested value object never declares a column.

mapping.description: Neutral storage mapping. `json` is the only value-object mapping defined in core; m-dialect maps it to each dialect's concrete storage type, such as Postgres jsonb, MariaDB json, or Snowflake VARIANT. Top-level only.

cardinality.description: The member's own cardinality: `one` for a single embedded document (the default), `many` for a JSON array of documents in the SAME column. Describes containment, not a relationship traversal — deliberately distinct from `relationship.cardinality`'s hyphenated join forms.

attributes.description: Typed fields of this value object. Unlike entity attributes they carry no per-field column: every attribute lives inside the one structured-document column.

valueObjects.description: Value objects nested inside this one, to arbitrary depth. Stored in the SAME structured-document column — never extra columns, rows, or joins.

nestedValueObject.description: A value object nested inside another value object. It shares its top-level ancestor's single structured-document column — never an extra column, row, or join — so it declares NO `column`/`mapping` storage properties (the closed object rejects them). Otherwise identical to a top-level value object: typed `attributes`, its own `one`/`many` cardinality, and further nested `valueObjects` to arbitrary depth.

cardinality.description: The nested member's own cardinality: `one` (default) for a single embedded document, `many` for a JSON array of documents inside the same column.

attributes.description: Typed fields of this nested value object; no per-field column.

valueObjects.description: Further-nested value objects, to arbitrary depth, in the same column.

valueObjectAttribute.description: A typed field of a value object. Unlike an entity `attribute` it carries NO per-field `column`: the whole value object (all attributes plus nested value objects) is stored in one structured-document column, so an inner field has no column of its own. Its declared m-core neutral type is normative for nested-predicate literal typing and casting (m-op-algebra/m-sql).

inheritance.description: Inheritance mapping for an entity that participates in a closed class hierarchy (m-inheritance). A hierarchy is a tree of entities under exactly one `root`; interior nodes are `abstract-subtype`s and leaves (or any instantiable node) are `concrete-subtype`s. The root ALONE declares the family `strategy`; descendants inherit it and MUST NOT redeclare it. Two strategies are admitted: `table-per-hierarchy` (the whole family in ONE shared table, rows discriminated by the root's `tag` column carrying each concrete subtype's `tagValue`) and `table-per-concrete-subtype` (each concrete subtype maps to its OWN table; no tag). `table-per-leaf` and `table-per-class` are NOT legal strategies and are REJECTED by this schema (the strategy enum omits both). The abstract `root` and every `abstract-subtype` are tableless and rowless (enforced by the entity-level `table` conditionals); only a `concrete-subtype` owns rows. Temporal axes (`asOfAttributes`, a per-entity field of the entity schema, not this `inheritance` block) are ALSO root-owned: only the root may declare them, and every descendant inherits the root's complete axis set unchanged (never redeclaring, adding, removing, overriding, or shadowing one) — a family-wide property, not an ordinary per-entity inherited member. Cross-entity family invariants (parent resolution, acyclicity, single root, concrete-under-abstract-root, family-wide `tagValue` uniqueness, shared-table consistency, full inherited attribute chain, temporal-axis root ownership) are semantic and validated above this per-entity schema.

strategy.description: The family mapping strategy, declared ONLY by the `root` (descendants inherit it). table-per-leaf and table-per-class are intentionally absent: the enum rejects them.

role.description: This entity's position in the closed tree: the abstract `root` that names the family and declares its strategy; an `abstract-subtype` interior node (tableless, rowless, addressable as a polymorphic position); or a `concrete-subtype`, the only instantiable, row-owning participant.

parent.description: For a non-root participant (`abstract-subtype` / `concrete-subtype`), the name of the entity it directly extends. REQUIRED for a non-root, FORBIDDEN for a root.

tag.description: table-per-hierarchy ROOT only: the discriminator column in the shared table distinguishing concrete subtypes. Declared once on the root (not repeated on descendants) and FORBIDDEN under table-per-concrete-subtype (no shared table). It is a framework-owned metadata column, NOT a declared entity attribute — abstract-target reads project it raw and materialize `familyVariant` from it (m-sql / m-case-format, resolved Q6).

column.description: The tag (discriminator) column name in the shared table.

tagValue.description: table-per-hierarchy CONCRETE-SUBTYPE only: the value the root's tag column carries for THIS concrete subtype's rows. Declared only on a `concrete-subtype`; whether it is REQUIRED depends on the family strategy (a cross-entity rule the root owns), so this per-entity schema leaves it optional and the semantic validator enforces its presence under table-per-hierarchy and its absence under table-per-concrete-subtype.

allOf.description: The `root` declares the family strategy and has NO parent; every non-root participant names its `parent` and does NOT redeclare the strategy.

The `tag` column lives on the table-per-hierarchy ROOT and nowhere else: REQUIRED on a `root` declaring `strategy: table-per-hierarchy`, FORBIDDEN otherwise (a table-per-concrete-subtype root, an abstract subtype, or a concrete subtype).
`tagValue` is a concrete subtype's discriminated value: only a `concrete-subtype` may declare it (a `root` / `abstract-subtype` MUST NOT).

pkGenerator.description: Primary-key generation strategy. Only the strategy keyword is required; sequence carries extra fields.
